title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'The world twenty years from now: What will technology change, and what will stay the same? Write an article exploring how you think technology will transform our lives over the next two decades — and whether those changes will be for better or worse.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
2044: A World We Can Barely Imagine

Predicting the future is a notoriously unreliable business. Twenty years ago, few foresaw how profoundly smartphones would transform daily life. Yet looking at the trajectory of current technologies, certain changes in the coming two decades seem not just possible but near-inevitable.

Artificial intelligence will almost certainly reshape the employment landscape, automating many routine cognitive tasks in the same way that machines once replaced manual labour. This need not be catastrophic — provided societies invest seriously in retraining and redesigning social support systems — but it will require significant adaptation.

Medical technology offers some of the most genuinely exciting prospects. Gene therapies, personalised medicine, and AI-assisted diagnostics are already transforming healthcare, and the pace of development shows no signs of slowing.

And yet, what will remain unchanged is equally interesting: our fundamental need for human connection, for meaning, for creativity. Technology can augment our capabilities, but it cannot replace what makes us human.

The question is not whether technology will change our lives — it will, profoundly. The question is whether we have the wisdom to shape those changes rather than simply submitting to them.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Riley has written to you saying they are feeling anxious about the future and about the rapid pace of change in the world. Write an email to Riley discussing your own hopes and concerns about the future, sharing your perspective on how to approach an uncertain world, and encouraging them to look forward with optimism.

Write your email to Riley.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Riley,

I really appreciate you sharing how you are feeling — honestly, I think a lot of people feel exactly the same way but are afraid to say so. The pace of change right now is genuinely bewildering, and it is entirely reasonable to feel uncertain about what lies ahead.

If I am honest, I have my own concerns too. Climate change worries me enormously, and I find the rate of technological disruption unsettling at times. I do not think pretending otherwise is helpful to either of us.

But here is what I keep coming back to: every generation has faced its version of an uncertain future, and human beings have shown a remarkable capacity to adapt, innovate, and find hope in the most difficult circumstances. History is full of problems that seemed insurmountable and were eventually solved.

I also find it genuinely encouraging that so many young people today are engaged, passionate, and determined to do better than previous generations. That energy matters.

Focus on what you can influence — your skills, your relationships, your choices. The rest will take shape around those.

You are not alone in this.

With love,
Alex

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You have been asked to write a review for an English-language entertainment website. Write a review of a science fiction film, book, or exhibition about the future that you have experienced recently. Describe what it explores, evaluate how thought-provoking and realistic its vision of the future is, and say whether you would recommend it.

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
'Echoes of Tomorrow' — A Science Fiction Film That Demands to Be Discussed

Science fiction at its best does not simply entertain — it holds a mirror up to the present by imagining the future. 'Echoes of Tomorrow', the acclaimed new film from director Naomi Vance, does precisely this with intelligence, visual ambition, and considerable emotional power.

Set in 2070, the film follows a climate refugee and a corporate AI engineer whose lives collide in a sprawling coastal megacity. It explores themes of inequality, environmental collapse, and digital identity with a seriousness that is rare in mainstream cinema. The production design is extraordinary, creating a future that feels genuinely plausible rather than fantastical — which makes its warnings all the more unsettling.

What elevates the film beyond spectacle is its insistence on human complexity. Neither its heroes nor its villains are straightforward, and the moral questions it raises — about responsibility, progress, and what we owe future generations — linger long after the credits roll.

If the pacing occasionally loses momentum in the second act, it is a minor complaint against a film of genuine substance.

I would strongly recommend 'Echoes of Tomorrow' to anyone prepared to leave the cinema thinking. Rating: 5 out of 5.
